17	// LockRetryPolicy describes the retry/backoff curve used when attempting to
18	// acquire a process-shared lock that guards git index operations on the
19	// primary .git (the tracker-commit lock). The policy is owned by this
20	// package per ddx-da11a34a AC#4: callers in cli/internal/triage and
21	// elsewhere consult ActionRetryWithBackoff but do not duplicate the curve
22	// implementation here.
23	//
24	// The curve is exponential with a cap and a bounded max-retries budget so
25	// that lock contention is absorbed silently up to a point, after which it
26	// surfaces as an ordinary error to the caller. Per TD-031 §8.5
27	// (LockContentionContract) lock contention does NOT change persisted bead
28	// status; the bead remains claimable and the worker remains `draining`.
29	type LockRetryPolicy struct {
30		// InitialBackoff is the wait before the second attempt.
31		InitialBackoff time.Duration
32		// MaxBackoff caps the per-step backoff after exponential growth.
33		MaxBackoff time.Duration
34		// Multiplier is the per-step growth factor (>= 1.0).
35		Multiplier float64
36		// MaxRetries bounds the number of acquisition attempts after the
37		// initial try. Total attempts = MaxRetries + 1.
38		MaxRetries int
39		// MaxElapsed bounds the total wall-clock time spent retrying. If both
40		// MaxRetries and MaxElapsed are set, the first to trip wins.
41		MaxElapsed time.Duration
42	}
43	
44	// DefaultLockRetryPolicy returns the policy used by withTrackerLock. The
45	// curve is intentionally tight (sub-second) for in-process tracker-commit
46	// contention; the larger 30s/90s/300s curve referenced by the auto-triage
47	// child of ddx-3c154349 lives one layer up, in cli/internal/triage —
48	// it consults ActionRetryWithBackoff and re-queues across drain passes,
49	// while this policy resolves contention within a single attempt.
50	func DefaultLockRetryPolicy() LockRetryPolicy {
51		return LockRetryPolicy{
52			InitialBackoff: 50 * time.Millisecond,
53			MaxBackoff:     1 * time.Second,
54			Multiplier:     2.0,
55			MaxRetries:     200,
56			MaxElapsed:     30 * time.Second,
57		}
58	}
59	
60	// step returns the backoff duration for retry index n (0-based: n=0 is the
61	// wait BEFORE the second acquisition attempt).
62	func (p LockRetryPolicy) step(n int) time.Duration {
63		if p.InitialBackoff <= 0 {
64			return 0
65		}
66		d := float64(p.InitialBackoff)
67		m := p.Multiplier
68		if m < 1 {
69			m = 1
70		}
71		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
72			d *= m
73			if p.MaxBackoff > 0 && d >= float64(p.MaxBackoff) {
74				d = float64(p.MaxBackoff)
75				break
76			}
77		}
78		out := time.Duration(d)
79		if p.MaxBackoff > 0 && out > p.MaxBackoff {
80			out = p.MaxBackoff
81		}
82		return out
83	}
84	
85	// trackerLockStaleAge is the age after which a held tracker lock is
86	// considered stale (owner crashed mid-commit) and may be forcibly broken.
87	var trackerLockStaleAge = 10 * time.Minute
88	
89	// trackerLockPolicy is the active retry policy for withTrackerLock.
90	// Tests may swap this out via the exported helpers below.
91	var trackerLockPolicy = DefaultLockRetryPolicy()
92	
93	// trackerLockPath returns the main-git lock directory for the given project
94	// root. The historical name remains because tests and older comments refer to
95	// the tracker lock, but the lock now protects all primary-checkout git writes:
96	// tracker commits, pre-dispatch checkpoint/ref updates, and landing.
97	//
98	// The lock target must be shared across linked worktrees for the same repo, so
99	// we prefer the primary DDx workspace when one exists instead of the caller's
100	// checkout-local .ddx directory.
101	func trackerLockPath(projectRoot string) string {
102		return filepath.Join(sharedMainGitLockRoot(projectRoot), ddxroot.DirName, ".git-tracker.lock")
103	}
104	
105	// sharedMainGitLockRoot resolves the DDx state root that should own the
106	// process-shared main-git lock. Linked worktrees should converge on the same
107	// primary workspace when one is available; otherwise we fall back to the
108	// normal project-scoped DDx path so convention-root projects keep their
109	// existing behavior.
110	func sharedMainGitLockRoot(projectRoot string) string {
111		if workspace := gitpkg.FindNearestDDxWorkspace(projectRoot); workspace != "" {
112			if info, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(workspace, ddxroot.DirName)); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
113				return workspace
114			}
115		}
116		return ddxroot.Path(context.Background(), projectRoot)
117	}
118	
119	// withMainGitLock acquires the process-shared main-git lock for the
120	// given project root, runs fn, and releases the lock. The lock is a
121	// directory created via os.Mkdir (atomic across processes on POSIX and
122	// Windows) following the same pattern as cli/internal/bead/lock.go.
123	//
124	// Contention is handled per LockRetryPolicy: exponential backoff up to a
125	// bounded budget. Per TD-031 §8.5 the contention itself does not change
126	// bead status; on budget exhaustion the caller surfaces an ordinary
127	// execution_failed and triage decides next steps.
128	func withMainGitLock(projectRoot string, fn func() error) error {
129		if projectRoot == "" {
130			return fn()
131		}
132		return withTrackerLockPolicy(projectRoot, trackerLockPolicy, fn)
133	}
134	
135	// WithMainGitLock exposes the process-shared main-git lock to sibling
136	// packages that need the same serialized add/commit boundary as execute-bead
137	// landing and pre-dispatch checkpointing.
138	func WithMainGitLock(projectRoot string, fn func() error) error {
139		return withMainGitLock(projectRoot, fn)
140	}
141	
142	// withTrackerLock is retained for existing tracker/pre-dispatch call sites. It
143	// uses the same process-shared lock as landing so separate ddx work processes
144	// cannot interleave commits, ref updates, and checkout sync in the main tree.
145	func withTrackerLock(projectRoot string, fn func() error) error {
146		return withMainGitLock(projectRoot, fn)
147	}
148	
149	// TrackerLockSample carries timing and contention metrics for one
150	// withTrackerLock / withMainGitLock acquire+release cycle.
151	type TrackerLockSample struct {
152		LockDir string
153		Wait    time.Duration // time from entry to lock acquisition
154		Hold    time.Duration // time the callback held the lock
155		Retries int           // number of sleep-and-retry iterations (0 = acquired on first try)
156	}
157	
158	// TrackerLockMetricsSink is called after each successful tracker-lock
159	// acquire+release cycle. The zero value (nil) is a no-op. Tests may swap
160	// this to capture metrics.
161	var TrackerLockMetricsSink func(TrackerLockSample)
162	
163	// trackerLockContendedAttemptHook is a test-only hook fired after each
164	// failed acquire attempt that has been classified as real lock contention
165	// (a directory exists at lockDir and the stale-owner path did not reclaim
166	// it). It is invoked just before the retry sleep with the zero-based
167	// attempt index. The zero value (nil) is a no-op.
168	//
169	// Tests use this hook to deterministically synchronise with the retry
170	// loop — e.g. release the held lock from the hook so the next attempt
171	// observes a non-zero retry count without relying on time.Sleep races.
172	var trackerLockContendedAttemptHook func(attempt int)
173	
174	// withTrackerLockPolicy is the policy-parameterised form of
175	// withTrackerLock; exposed at package scope so tests can pin a specific
176	// curve.
177	func withTrackerLockPolicy(projectRoot string, policy LockRetryPolicy, fn func() error) error {
178		lockDir := trackerLockPath(projectRoot)
179		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(lockDir), 0o755); err != nil {
180			return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock dir: %w", err)
181		}
182	
183		start := time.Now()
184		retries := 0
185		for attempt := 0; ; attempt++ {
186			err := os.Mkdir(lockDir, 0o755)
187			if err == nil {
188				_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "pid"),
189					[]byte(fmt.Sprintf("%d", os.Getpid())), 0o644)
190				_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "acquired_at"),
191					[]byte(time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)), 0o644)
192				break
193			}
194	
195			// Classify what exists at lockDir before deciding to sleep or fail.
196			// Per TD-031 §8.5: only a confirmed lock directory enters retry/backoff;
197			// malformed paths are operator diagnostics, not lock contention.
198			info, statErr := os.Lstat(lockDir)
199			if statErr != nil {
200				if os.IsNotExist(statErr) {
201					// Path disappeared between Mkdir and Lstat (race). Retry immediately.
202					continue
203				}
204				return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock stat: %w", statErr)
205			}
206	
207			switch {
208			case info.Mode().IsDir():
209				// Real lock directory. Apply existing stale-owner policy.
210				if breakStaleTrackerLock(lockDir) {
211					continue
212				}
213				if attempt >= policy.MaxRetries {
214					return lockTimeoutError(lockDir, "max retries")
215				}
216				if policy.MaxElapsed > 0 && time.Since(start) >= policy.MaxElapsed {
217					return lockTimeoutError(lockDir, "max elapsed")
218				}
219				retries++
220				if hook := trackerLockContendedAttemptHook; hook != nil {
221					hook(attempt)
222				}
223				time.Sleep(policy.step(attempt))
224	
225			case info.Mode().IsRegular():
226				// Malformed: lock path must be a directory, not a regular file.
227				if time.Since(info.ModTime()) > trackerLockStaleAge {
228					// Stale regular file: remove with single-path removal and retry immediately.
229					if rmErr := os.Remove(lockDir); rmErr != nil && !os.IsNotExist(rmErr) {
230						return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock: remove malformed stale file %s: %w", lockDir, rmErr)
231					}
232					continue
233				}
234				return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock: malformed lock path %s is a regular file; a lock directory was expected — remove it manually to recover", lockDir)
235	
236			default:
237				// Symlink, socket, device, or other special filesystem object. Do not remove.
238				return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock: malformed lock path %s has type %v; a lock directory was expected — inspect and remove it manually to recover", lockDir, info.Mode().Type())
239			}
240		}
241	
242		waitDur := time.Since(start)
243		holdStart := time.Now()
244		defer os.RemoveAll(lockDir)
245		fnErr := lockmetrics.Instrument("tracker.lock", "tracker.commit", fn)
246		if sink := TrackerLockMetricsSink; sink != nil {
247			sink(TrackerLockSample{LockDir: lockDir, Wait: waitDur, Hold: time.Since(holdStart), Retries: retries})
248		}
249		return fnErr
250	}
251	
252	func lockTimeoutError(lockDir, why string) error {
253		owner := "missing"
254		if pidData, perr := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "pid")); perr == nil && len(pidData) > 0 {
255			owner = strings.TrimSpace(string(pidData))
256		}
257		return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock timeout (%s, lock: %s, owner pid: %s)", why, lockDir, owner)
258	}
259	
260	// breakStaleTrackerLock removes lockDir if its owner process is dead or the
261	// lock is older than trackerLockStaleAge. Returns true if the lock was broken.
262	func breakStaleTrackerLock(lockDir string) bool {
263		pidData, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "pid"))
264		if err == nil {
265			pid, err := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(string(pidData)))
266			if err == nil && pid > 0 && pid != os.Getpid() {
267				if !trackerProcessAlive(pid) {
268					os.RemoveAll(lockDir)
269					return true
270				}
271			}
272		}
273	
274		acquiredData, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "acquired_at"))
275		if err == nil {
276			acquired, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, strings.TrimSpace(string(acquiredData)))
277			if err == nil && time.Since(acquired) > trackerLockStaleAge {
278				os.RemoveAll(lockDir)
279				return true
280			}
281		}
282	
283		return false
284	}

29	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
30	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, powerClass bounds, etc.) live on
31	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
32	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
33		Log             io.Writer

36		ResourceChecker ExecutionResourceChecker

38		CleanupTickCh   <-chan time.Time
39		EventSink       io.Writer
40		ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent
41		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
42		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
43		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
44		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
45		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
46		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
47		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook

49		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)

57		// large for implementation-level splitting. The hook should run the same
58		// orchestrator splitter used by the intake gate and return child specs and an
59		// AC map. A nil return or error falls back to operator-required parking.
60		PostAttemptDecompositionHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (*PreClaimDecomposition, error)
61		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
62		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
63		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
64		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
65		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
66		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
67		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
68		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
69		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is

159	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
160		mode := r.Mode
161		if mode == "" {
162			if r.Once {
163				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
164			} else {
165				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
166			}
167		}
168		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
169		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
170			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
171		}
172		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
173			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
174		}
175		return mode, idleInterval
176	}

181	// holding a lease (ddx-8f2e0ebf); 60s is short enough to release promptly and
182	// long enough not to false-trip a slow-but-healthy resolver.
183	const DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout = 60 * time.Second

186	// timeout, falling back to DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout when unset.
187	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) effectiveRouteResolutionTimeout() time.Duration {

247			return r.PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold
248		}
249		return DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold
250	}

252	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
253	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
254	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
255	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
256	
257	// ReviewerSkippedEmptyDiffEventKind is the event kind emitted when the
258	// reviewer dispatch is skipped because the implementer produced no commits
259	// (BaseRev == ResultRev). Per ADR-024 P1: cheapest first — paying a reviewer
260	// to evaluate an empty diff is unjustified cost.
261	const ReviewerSkippedEmptyDiffEventKind = "reviewer-skipped-empty-diff"
262	
263	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the work will set for
264	// any work-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
265	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
266	// `ddx bead update --set work-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
267	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
268	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
269	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
270	
271	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
272	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
273	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
274	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
275	// advance the base branch.
276	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
277	
278	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
279	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
280	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
281	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
282	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
283	// worker.
284	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
285	
286	// ProviderUnavailableCooldown is the retry pause for route/provider outages
287	// where no configured powerClass can currently run the bead. This is transient
288	// scheduler state, not bead quality evidence, so it must not become a durable
289	// triage:needs-investigation park.
290	const ProviderUnavailableCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
291	
292	// SmartRetryCooldown is the queue-fairness pause after a real no_changes
293	// implementation attempt asks for status=open/smart retry. The bead remains
294	// open with transient smart-retry metadata, but a watch worker moves on instead
295	// of repeating the same empty-diff attempt every idle cycle.
296	const SmartRetryCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
297	
298	// PausedInfraInterval is the polling interval the worker waits in paused-infra
299	// state before re-evaluating the queue. Infra failures (no_viable_provider,
300	// provider_connectivity) transition the worker to this state instead of parking
301	// individual beads: beads remain immediately reclaimable, and the worker retries
302	// after this window.
303	const PausedInfraInterval = 2 * time.Minute
304	
305	// SubmitWithPreMergeChecks is the canonical land-back step for the execute-bead
306	// loop. It runs the project's .ddx/checks/*.yaml gate against the worker's
307	// (baseRev, resultRev) before forwarding to submit. On Blocked, it preserves
308	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked
309	// events on the bead, and returns the synthesised LandResult{Status:"preserved"}
310	// without ever calling submit. On pass, honoured checks-bypass annotations are
311	// recorded as checks-bypass events and submit runs normally.
312	//
313	// submit is the project's land coordinator entry point (typically
314	// LocalLandCoordinator.Submit or the server-side coordinator submit).
315	//
316	// The implementation lives in checks_premerge.go alongside its supporting
317	// helpers (RunPreMergeChecks, AppendPreMergeChecksEvents,
318	// PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks); this thin wrapper exists so the execute-bead
319	// loop's land-back call site is one obvious helper rather than three nested
320	// ones, and so callers wiring the gate do not need to import the helper layer
321	// directly.
322	func SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
323		ctx context.Context,
324		projectRoot string,
325		b *bead.Bead,
326		res *ExecuteBeadResult,
327		submit func(LandRequest) (*LandResult, error),
328		eventStore BeadEventAppender,
329		actor, source string,
330		now func() time.Time,
331	) (*LandResult, *PreMergeChecksOutcome, error) {
332		if now == nil {
333			now = time.Now
334		}
335		if submit == nil {
336			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("submit-with-pre-merge-checks: submit callback required")
337		}
338		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir)
339		outcome, err := RunPreMergeChecks(ctx, projectRoot, b, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev, evidenceDir)
340		if err != nil {
341			// Treat checks_bypass / loader errors as a hard preserve: the worker
342			// did its job; the operator misconfigured the gate. Better to park
343			// the result under an iteration ref than to silently land it.
344			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
345			if upErr := (&RealOrchestratorGitOps{}).UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); upErr != nil {
346				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result after checks setup error: %w (original error: %v)", upErr, err)
347			}
348			return &LandResult{
349				Status:      "preserved",
350				PreserveRef: ref,
351				Reason:      "pre-merge checks setup error: " + err.Error(),
352			}, nil, nil
353		}
354		if outcome.Blocked {
355			land, perr := PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks(projectRoot, res, outcome, &RealOrchestratorGitOps{})
356			if perr != nil {
357				return nil, outcome, perr
358			}
359			if eventStore != nil {
360				_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
361			}
362			return land, outcome, nil
363		}
364		if eventStore != nil && len(outcome.Bypassed) > 0 {
365			_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
366		}
367		var submitted *LandResult
368		cfg, cfgErr := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
369		_, landErr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}, BeadLandingOptions{
370			LandingAdvancer: func(res *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
371				req := BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, res)
372				if cfgErr == nil && cfg != nil && cfg.Git != nil && len(cfg.Git.PostLandCommand) > 0 {
373					req.PostLandCommand = append([]string(nil), cfg.Git.PostLandCommand...)
374				}
375				land, err := submit(req)
376				if err == nil {
377					submitted = land
378				}
379				return land, err
380			},
381		})
382		return submitted, outcome, landErr
383	}

385	// handlePostAttemptDecomposition runs the orchestrator-level splitter when a
386	// no_changes attempt signals orchestrator_action: decompose. It checks the
387	// queue-level max_decomposition_depth (not the implementation prompt cap),
388	// validates the AC map for completeness, and either creates children+deps or
389	// parks the parent for operator review if the split is lossy or depth-capped.
390	// The bead must already be unclaimed before this is called.
391	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handlePostAttemptDecomposition(ctx context.Context, candidate *bead.Bead, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, at time.Time) {
392		emit := func(kind string, body map[string]any) {
393			if runtime.EventSink == nil {
394				return
395			}
396			data, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{"event": kind, "payload": body})
397			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.EventSink, "%s\n", data)
398		}
399		parkOperator := func(reason string) {
400			if runtime.Log != nil {
401				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition blocked: %s (%s)\n", reason, candidate.ID)
402			}
403			emit("post_attempt_decomposition.blocked", map[string]any{
404				"bead_id": candidate.ID,
405				"reason":  reason,
406			})
407			_, berr := parkBeadPostIntakeRejection(w.Store, candidate, assignee, PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired, "operator_required", reason, at)
408			if berr != nil {
409				if runtime.Log != nil {
410					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition park error: %v\n", berr)
411				}
412			}
413		}
414	
415		// Queue-level depth check: orchestrator uses its own cap, not the
416		// implementation prompt's hardcoded depth-2 cap.
417		maxDepth := rcfg.MaxDecompositionDepth()
418		if maxDepth > 0 && storeBeadDepth(ctx, w.Store, candidate) >= maxDepth {
419			overflowBody, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
420				"depth": storeBeadDepth(ctx, w.Store, candidate),
421				"max":   maxDepth,
422			})
423			_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
424				Kind:      "triage-overflow",
425				Summary:   "depth cap exceeded during post-attempt decomposition",
426				Body:      string(overflowBody),
427				Actor:     assignee,
428				Source:    "ddx work",
429				CreatedAt: at,
430			})
431			parkOperator("queue-level depth cap exceeded; operator must split")
432			return
433		}
434	
435		decomp, err := runtime.PostAttemptDecompositionHook(ctx, candidate.ID)
436		if err != nil {
437			parkOperator(fmt.Sprintf("decomposition hook error: %s", err.Error()))
438			return
439		}
440		if decomp == nil {
441			parkOperator("decomposition hook returned no split")
442			return
443		}
444		lossyOrEmpty := isDecompositionLossy(decomp.ACMap) || (len(decomp.ACMap) == 0 && strings.TrimSpace(candidate.Acceptance) != "")
445		if lossyOrEmpty {
446			parkOperator("decomposition AC map is incomplete; operator must produce a lossless split")
447			return
448		}
449	
450		childIDs, decompErr := applyPreClaimDecomposition(ctx, w.Store, candidate, decomp, assignee, at)
451		if decompErr != nil {
452			parkOperator(fmt.Sprintf("decomposition apply error: %s", decompErr.Error()))
453			return
454		}
455		if runtime.Log != nil {
456			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition: bead %s split into %s\n", candidate.ID, strings.Join(childIDs, ", "))
457		}
458		emit("post_attempt_decomposition.applied", map[string]any{
459			"bead_id":   candidate.ID,
460			"child_ids": childIDs,
461		})
462	}

464	// runPostAttemptTriage invokes the optional post-attempt triage hook after
465	// the attempt outcome has been finalized but before any cooldown decision is
466	// applied. Hook failures are fail-open: the report is returned unchanged and
467	// legacy cooldown behavior remains in force.
468	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) runPostAttemptTriage(ctx context.Context, candidate bead.Bead, report ExecuteBeadReport, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, now func() time.Time) ExecuteBeadReport {
469		hook := runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook
470		if hook == nil {
471			return report
472		}
473		if isDeterministicSystemOutcomeReason(report.OutcomeReason) {
474			return report
475		}
476		triage, err := hook(ctx, candidate.ID, report)
477		if err != nil {
478			if runtime.Log != nil {
479				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, err)
480			}
481			return report
482		}
483		triage = normalizeTriageResult(triage)
484		hasWarnings := len(triage.DecodeWarnings) > 0 || triage.Malformed
485		classificationRecognized := isRecognizedTriageClassification(triage.Classification)
486		if hasWarnings && runtime.Log != nil {
487			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage warning (%s %s): %s (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, formatTriageWarnings(triage.DecodeWarnings))
488		}
489		if !classificationRecognized && !hasWarnings {
490			return report
491		}
492		if classificationRecognized {
493			report.OutcomeReason = triage.Classification
494		}
495		recordPostAttemptTriageEvent(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, triage, assignee, now().UTC())
496		return report
497	}

511	// CapLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
512	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
513	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
514	func CapLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
515		if d <= 0 {
516			return 0
517		}
518		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
519			return MaxLoopCooldown
520		}
521		return d
522	}

940	func danglingSuccessRecoveryReport(beadID string, recovery *DanglingSuccessRecovery) ExecuteBeadReport {
941		report := ExecuteBeadReport{BeadID: beadID}
942		if recovery == nil {
943			report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
944			report.Detail = "dangling-success recovery returned no terminal outcome"
945			report.OutcomeReason = "operator_required"
946			return report
947		}
948		report.AttemptID = recovery.AttemptID
949		report.SessionID = recovery.SessionID
950		report.BaseRev = recovery.BaseRev
951		report.ResultRev = recovery.ResultRev
952		report.PreserveRef = recovery.PreserveRef
953	
954		switch recovery.Outcome {
955		case danglingSuccessOutcomeClosed:
956			report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
957			report.Detail = "dangling-success recovery: bead closed idempotently (result_rev already merged)"
958		case danglingSuccessOutcomePreserved:
959			report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
960			report.Detail = "dangling-success: preserved successful result for operator landing"
961		case danglingSuccessOutcomeOperatorRequired:
962			report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
963			report.OutcomeReason = "operator_required"
964			if recovery.FailureReason != "" {
965				report.Detail = "dangling-success: successful result could not be landed automatically: " + recovery.FailureReason
966			} else {
967				report.Detail = "dangling-success: successful result could not be landed automatically"
968			}
969		default:
970			report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
971			report.OutcomeReason = "operator_required"
972			report.Detail = "dangling-success: unrecognized recovery outcome"
973		}
974		return report
975	}

977	const preExecuteCheckpointDirtyMarker = "pre-execute-bead checkpoint: " + preDispatchCheckpointDirtyRefusalPrefix

979	func preExecuteCheckpointDirtyStop(report ExecuteBeadReport, err error, projectRoot, beadID string) (*OperatorAttentionStop, bool) {
980		detail := strings.TrimSpace(firstNonEmpty(report.Detail, report.Error))
981		if err != nil && strings.Contains(err.Error(), preExecuteCheckpointDirtyMarker) {
982			detail = strings.TrimSpace(err.Error())
983		} else if !strings.Contains(detail, preExecuteCheckpointDirtyMarker) {
984			return nil, false
985		}
986		paths := parsePreExecuteCheckpointDirtyPaths(detail)
987		message := "commit or clean the listed implementation files before restarting ddx work"
988		if len(paths) > 0 {
989			message = fmt.Sprintf("commit or clean the listed implementation files before restarting ddx work: %s", strings.Join(paths, ", "))
990		}
991		return &OperatorAttentionStop{
992			Reason:      "checkpoint_dirty",
993			BeadID:      beadID,
994			ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
995			DirtyPaths:  paths,
996			Message:     message,
997		}, true
998	}

1000	func parsePreExecuteCheckpointDirtyPaths(detail string) []string {
1001		idx := strings.Index(detail, preDispatchCheckpointDirtyRefusalPrefix)
1002		if idx == -1 {
1003			return nil
1004		}
1005		pathsField := detail[idx+len(preDispatchCheckpointDirtyRefusalPrefix):]
1006		if semi := strings.Index(pathsField, ";"); semi >= 0 {
1007			pathsField = pathsField[:semi]
1008		}
1009		var paths []string
1010		for _, path := range strings.Split(pathsField, ",") {
1011			path = strings.TrimSpace(path)
1012			if path != "" {
1013				paths = append(paths, path)
1014			}
1015		}
1016		return paths
1017	}

1019	// isTransientGitContention reports git index/ref contention errors that are
1020	// transient under concurrent workers and must be retried rather than treated
1021	// as a worker-stopping failure (ddx-23ac2796 and its sibling variants). It
1022	// covers .git/index.lock conflicts plus the "unable to write new index file"
1023	// and "cannot lock ref" forms git emits when a concurrent process holds the
1024	// index/ref. A persistent cause (e.g. ENOSPC) simply keeps releasing+retrying
1025	// the bead, which is still preferable to halting the whole unattended drain.
1026	func isTransientGitContention(msg string) bool {
1027		if gitlock.IsIndexLockError(msg) {
1028			return true
1029		}
1030		lower := strings.ToLower(msg)
1031		for _, marker := range []string{
1032			"unable to write new index file",
1033			"cannot lock ref",
1034			"unable to update the ref",
1035		} {
1036			if strings.Contains(lower, marker) {
1037				return true
1038			}
1039		}
1040		return false
1041	}

1043	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
1044		if w.Store == nil {
1045			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
1046		}
1047		if w.Executor == nil {
1048			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
1049		}
1050	
1051		now := w.Now
1052		if now == nil {
1053			now = time.Now
1054		}
1055		assignee := rcfg.Assignee()
1056		if assignee == "" {
1057			assignee = "ddx"
1058		}
1059		preClaimTimeout := runtime.PreClaimTimeout
1060		if preClaimTimeout <= 0 {
1061			preClaimTimeout = work.DefaultPreClaimTimeout
1062		}
1063		noProgressCooldown := rcfg.NoProgressCooldown()
1064		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
1065			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
1066		}
1067		heartbeatInterval := rcfg.HeartbeatInterval()
1068		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
1069			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
1070		}
1071		harness := rcfg.Harness()
1072		provider := rcfg.Provider()
1073		model := rcfg.Model()
1074		profile := rcfg.Profile()
1075		loopMode, idleInterval := runtime.loopIntent()
1076	
1077		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
1078		resultsResetIdx := 0
1079		transientCandidateSkips := map[string]string{}
1080		w.transientCandidateSkips = transientCandidateSkips
1081		defer func() {
1082			w.transientCandidateSkips = nil
1083		}()
1084		// pausedInfraUntil is set when a no_viable_provider outcome is recorded.
1085		// The worker sleeps until this time (in Watch mode) before re-evaluating
1086		// the queue, leaving all beads immediately reclaimable. Zero means no pause.
1087		var pausedInfraUntil time.Time
1088		complexityGuard := work.NewComplexityGuard(w.ComplexityGate, runtime.Log)
1089		preclaimGuard := work.NewPreClaimGuard(runtime.PreClaimHook, w.Store, runtime.Log, now, 30*time.Second, preClaimTimeout)
1090		workLog := NewWorkLogRenderer(WorkLogRendererOptions{Now: now})
1091		wasIdle := false
1092		lastIdleQueueSignature := ""
1093	
1094		// Pre-claim idle escalation state (ddx-df77e668 AC #3). When the worker
1095		// idles on the same pre-claim blocker for preClaimIdleEscalationThreshold
1096		// consecutive cycles, it emits a non-terminal operator-attention event so
1097		// the stall is visible instead of looping silently. The streak resets when
1098		// the blocker detail changes or the worker stops idling on it.
1099		preClaimIdleDetail := ""
1100		preClaimIdleStreak := 0
1101		preClaimIdleFirstAt := time.Time{}
1102		preClaimIdleEscalated := false
1103		resetPreClaimIdleStreak := func() {
1104			preClaimIdleDetail = ""
1105			preClaimIdleStreak = 0
1106			preClaimIdleEscalated = false
1107		}
1108	
1109		// Worker-side liveness sidecar. Updated on each heartbeat tick so an
1110		// operator's `ddx work focus` / GraphQL panel can answer "is this worker
1111		// alive and what is it doing?" without paying tracker rewrite cost. The
1112		// sidecar is best-effort: write/sink failures do not interrupt the loop.
1113		// The reporter is constructed here but does NOT emit until a bead is
1114		// claimed — that order keeps loop.start as the first envelope on the
1115		// EventSink so structured-event consumers continue to see it.
1116		var liveness *work.SidecarLivenessReporter
1117		if runtime.ProjectRoot != "" && runtime.SessionID != "" {
1118			liveness = work.NewSidecarLivenessReporter(runtime.ProjectRoot, runtime.SessionID, runtime.SessionID, runtime.EventSink)
1119		}
1120	
1121		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
1122			writeLoopEvent(runtime.EventSink, runtime.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
1123		}
1124		claimSuccessRateWindowSize := runtime.effectiveClaimSuccessRateWindow()
1125		claimSuccessRateThreshold := runtime.effectiveClaimSuccessRateThreshold()
1126		claimSuccessRateWindow := make([]bool, 0, claimSuccessRateWindowSize)
1127		claimSuccessRateSuccesses := 0
1128		claimSuccessRateWarned := false
1129		preClaimWarnThreshold := runtime.effectivePreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold()
1130		preClaimWarnState := preClaimWarnRepeatState{}
1131		// exitReason is populated as the loop exits to surface a structured reason
1132		// in the loop.end event (ddx-dc157075 AC #4). Work-owned terminal reasons
1133		// are classified through work.StopCondition; fatal_config,
1134		// preflight_failed, resource_exhausted, and future provider exhaustion are
1135		// still subsystem-specific exits.
1136		exitReason := ""
1137		setExit := func(condition, reason string) {
1138			exitReason = reason
1139			result.StopCondition = condition
1140			result.ExitReason = reason
1141		}
1142		appendPreClaimWarn := func(beadID, reason, detail string, at time.Time) bool {
1143			escalated := appendPreClaimIntakeWarning(w.Store, emit, &preClaimWarnState, preClaimWarnThreshold, beadID, assignee, reason, detail, at)
1144			if !escalated {
1145				return false
1146			}
1147			detailText := fmt.Sprintf(
1148				"pre-claim warn fingerprint repeated %d times across %d distinct bead IDs",
1149				preClaimWarnState.Count,
1150				len(preClaimWarnState.DistinctBeadIDs),
1151			)
1152			result.OperatorAttention = &OperatorAttentionStop{
1153				Reason:      "preclaim_warn_repeated",
1154				BeadID:      beadID,
1155				ProjectRoot: runtime.ProjectRoot,
1156				Message:     detailText,
1157			}
1158			setExit("OperatorAttention", "operator_attention")
1159			if runtime.Log != nil {
1160				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "operator attention: %s\n", detailText)
1161			}
1162			return true
1163		}
1164		recordClaimAttempt := func(success bool, beadID string) {
1165			if claimSuccessRateWindowSize <= 0 {
1166				return
1167			}
1168			if len(claimSuccessRateWindow) == claimSuccessRateWindowSize {
1169				if claimSuccessRateWindow[0] {
1170					claimSuccessRateSuccesses--
1171				}
1172				copy(claimSuccessRateWindow, claimSuccessRateWindow[1:])
1173				claimSuccessRateWindow = claimSuccessRateWindow[:claimSuccessRateWindowSize-1]
1174			}
1175			claimSuccessRateWindow = append(claimSuccessRateWindow, success)
1176			if success {
1177				claimSuccessRateSuccesses++
1178				claimSuccessRateWarned = false
1179				return
1180			}
1181			if len(claimSuccessRateWindow) < claimSuccessRateWindowSize {
1182				return
1183			}
1184			rate := float64(claimSuccessRateSuccesses) / float64(claimSuccessRateWindowSize)
1185			if rate > claimSuccessRateThreshold || claimSuccessRateWarned {
1186				return
1187			}
1188			claimSuccessRateWarned = true
1189			detail := fmt.Sprintf(
1190				"claim success rate %.3f over the last %d claim attempts (%d successes, %d non-successes) is at or below threshold %.3f; manual intervention may be required",
1191				rate,
1192				claimSuccessRateWindowSize,
1193				claimSuccessRateSuccesses,
1194				claimSuccessRateWindowSize-claimSuccessRateSuccesses,
1195				claimSuccessRateThreshold,
1196			)
1197			emit("loop.operator_attention", map[string]any{
1198				"reason":        "claim_success_rate_below_threshold",
1199				"bead_id":       beadID,
1200				"detail":        detail,
1201				"window_size":   claimSuccessRateWindowSize,
1202				"successes":     claimSuccessRateSuccesses,
1203				"non_successes": claimSuccessRateWindowSize - claimSuccessRateSuccesses,
1204				"success_rate":  rate,
1205				"threshold":     claimSuccessRateThreshold,
1206			})
1207			if runtime.Log != nil {
1208				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "operator attention: %s\n", detail)
1209			}
1210		}
1211		clearTransientCandidateSkips := func() {
1212			for beadID := range transientCandidateSkips {
1213				delete(transientCandidateSkips, beadID)
1214			}
1215		}
1216		stopAfterNonAttemptSkip := func() bool {
1217			return loopMode == executeloop.ModeOnce && runtime.TargetBeadID != ""
1218		}
1219		strictIntakeBlocking := func() bool {
1220			return runtime.TargetBeadID != ""
1221		}
1222	
1223		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
1224			"worker_id":    runtime.WorkerID,
1225			"project_root": runtime.ProjectRoot,
1226			"harness":      harness,
1227			"model":        model,
1228			"session_id":   runtime.SessionID,
1229			"assignee":     assignee,
1230			"mode":         string(loopMode),
1231			"once":         loopMode == executeloop.ModeOnce,
1232		})
1233		cleanupLog := runtime.CleanupLog
1234		if cleanupLog == nil {
1235			cleanupLog = runtime.Log
1236		}
1237		cleanupStop := startExecutionCleanupWorker(ctx, runtime.ProjectRoot, runtime.CleanupRunner, runtime.CleanupInterval, runtime.CleanupTickCh, cleanupLog, emit)
1238		attemptStarted := false
1239		defer func() {
1240			cleanupStop(ctx.Err() != nil && attemptStarted)
1241		}()
1242		_, _, _ = runExecutionCleanupPass(ctx, runtime.ProjectRoot, runtime.CleanupRunner, cleanupLog, emit, "startup")
1243		leaseReader, _ := w.Store.(orphanHarnessLeaseReader)
1244		releaser, _ := w.Store.(orphanHarnessLeaseReleaser)
1245		appender, _ := w.Store.(orphanHarnessEventAppender)
1246		if reaped, reapErr := reapOrphanedHarnessChildren(
1247			ctx,
1248			runtime.ProjectRoot,
1249			newOrphanHarnessProcessScanner(),
1250			leaseReader,
1251			releaser,
1252			appender,
1253			assignee,
1254			runtime.Log,
1255			emit,
1256			func(pid int) error { return killProcessGroup(pid) },
1257		); reapErr != nil {
1258			if runtime.Log != nil {
1259				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "startup orphan harness reaper failed: %v\n", reapErr)
1260			}
1261			emit("loop.operator_attention", map[string]any{
1262				"reason":       "orphan_harness_reaper_failed",
1263				"project_root": runtime.ProjectRoot,
1264				"error":        reapErr.Error(),
1265			})

75	// Without withTrackerLock around the git add/commit pair, one of the two
76	// goroutines would fail with: "fatal: Unable to create '.../.git/index.lock':
77	// File exists." (See bead description for the observed Phase 2 drain failure.)
78	func TestTrackerCommit_ConcurrentSafety(t *testing.T) {
79		root := initTrackerRepo(t)
80		tracker := filepath.Join(root, ddxroot.DirName, "beads.jsonl")
81	
82		const goroutines = 2
83		const iterations = 8
84	
85		var wg sync.WaitGroup
86		errs := make(chan error, goroutines*iterations)
87		// mu guards file writes so the on-disk content is well-defined; the tested
88		// race is the git-index race inside CommitTracker, not file-write ordering.
89		var mu sync.Mutex
90		counter := 0
91	
92		for g := 0; g < goroutines; g++ {
93			wg.Add(1)
94			go func(g int) {
95				defer wg.Done()
96				for i := 0; i < iterations; i++ {
97					mu.Lock()
98					counter++
99					line := fmt.Sprintf(`{"id":"ddx-test-%04d-%04d","n":%d}`+"\n", g, i, counter)
100					f, err := os.OpenFile(tracker, os.O_APPEND|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
101					if err != nil {

103						errs <- fmt.Errorf("open tracker: %w", err)
104						return
105					}

107					_ = f.Close()
108					mu.Unlock()

110					if err := commitTrackerForTest(root); err != nil {
111						errs <- err
112						return
113					}
114				}
115			}(g)

118		close(errs)

120		for err := range errs {

553	func TestTrackerLock_RecordsRetryCount(t *testing.T) {
554		root := initTrackerRepo(t)
555	
556		var mu sync.Mutex
557		var captured []TrackerLockSample
558		prev := TrackerLockMetricsSink
559		TrackerLockMetricsSink = func(s TrackerLockSample) {
560			mu.Lock()
561			captured = append(captured, s)
562			mu.Unlock()
563		}
564		defer func() { TrackerLockMetricsSink = prev }()
565	
566		// Pre-acquire the lock with the current PID so breakStaleTrackerLock
567		// cannot reclaim it.

573			[]byte(time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)), 0o644))
574	
575		// Deterministic contention: release the held lock from the retry
576		// loop's contended-attempt hook on the first failed Mkdir. This
577		// guarantees Retries >= 1 regardless of scheduler load (a previous
578		// time.Sleep(30ms) based release was flaky under parallel-package
579		// load when the releaser goroutine ran before withTrackerLockPolicy
580		// even reached its Mkdir call).
581		var releaseOnce sync.Once
582		prevHook := trackerLockContendedAttemptHook
583		trackerLockContendedAttemptHook = func(int) {
584			releaseOnce.Do(func() {
585				_ = os.RemoveAll(lockDir)
586			})
587		}
588		defer func() { trackerLockContendedAttemptHook = prevHook }()

602		mu.Unlock()

618	// TestTrackerLock_StaleLockAfterPartialLocalLand verifies that a worker
619	// that acquires withMainGitLock, successfully calls UpdateRefTo (advancing
620	// local main), and then crashes without releasing the lock, can be recovered
621	// by breakStaleTrackerLock so a subsequent worker can proceed.
622	func TestTrackerLock_StaleLockAfterPartialLocalLand(t *testing.T) {
623		r := newLandTestRepo(t)
624		ops := RealLandingGitOps{}
625	
626		// Create a second, independent commit branch to land.
627		landSHA := r.commitOn(r.baseSHA, "worker.txt", "worker content\n", "feat: worker change")
628	
629		// First phase: simulate a worker that advances local main via UpdateRefTo,
630		// then crash (by leaving the lock in place). We do this in a controlled way
631		// inside a withMainGitLock block so we can verify UpdateRefTo succeeded.
632		lockDir := trackerLockPath(r.dir)
633		updateRefSucceeded := make(chan struct{})
634		lockAcquired := make(chan struct{})
635	
636		go func() {
637			_ = withMainGitLock(r.dir, func() error {
638				close(lockAcquired)
639	
640				// Worker acquired the lock. Perform UpdateRefTo to advance main
641				// locally (simulating the fast-path in Land).
642				targetRef := "refs/heads/main"
643				if err := ops.UpdateRefTo(r.dir, targetRef, landSHA, r.baseSHA); err != nil {
644					return fmt.Errorf("UpdateRefTo failed: %w", err)
645				}
646	
647				// Signal that UpdateRefTo succeeded while we hold the lock.
648				close(updateRefSucceeded)
649	
650				// Now simulate a crash by panicking. This will escape the
651				// withMainGitLock's defer cleanup because panic unwinds the stack
652				// differently. To properly simulate this without actually panicking
653				// (which would fail the test), we manually remove the defer cleanup
654				// by manipulating the lock after returning from this function.
655				return nil
656			})
657	
658			// After exiting withMainGitLock, the defer will have cleaned up the lock.
659			// We simulate the crash scenario by recreating the stale lock.
660			_ = os.MkdirAll(lockDir, 0o755)
661			_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "pid"), []byte("99999"), 0o644)
662			staleTime := time.Now().Add(-2 * trackerLockStaleAge).UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
663			_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "acquired_at"), []byte(staleTime), 0o644)
664		}()
665	
666		// Wait for the first worker to acquire the lock and advance main.
667		select {
668		case <-lockAcquired:
669		case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
670			t.Fatal("worker did not acquire lock in time")
671		}
672	
673		select {
674		case <-updateRefSucceeded:
675		case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
676			t.Fatal("UpdateRefTo did not complete in time")
677		}
678	
679		// Give the first goroutine time to recreate the stale lock.
680		time.Sleep(10 * time.Millisecond)
681	
682		// Verify that main has been advanced locally.
683		mainTip, err := ops.ResolveRef(r.dir, "refs/heads/main")
684		require.NoError(t, err)
685		if mainTip != landSHA {
686			t.Fatalf("main not advanced by worker: tip = %s, want = %s", mainTip, landSHA)
687		}
688	
689		// Verify that the stale lock is in place.
690		if _, err := os.Stat(lockDir); err != nil {
691			t.Fatalf("lock dir should exist: %v", err)
692		}
693	
694		// Second worker arrives and attempts to acquire the lock. It should
695		// observe the stale lock, break it, and proceed with its work.
696		secondWorkerErr := make(chan error, 1)
697		go func() {
698			secondWorkerErr <- withMainGitLock(r.dir, func() error {
699				// Verify that we can see the local-ahead state (main is already at landSHA).
700				tip, err := ops.ResolveRef(r.dir, "refs/heads/main")
701				if err != nil {
702					return fmt.Errorf("resolving main: %w", err)
703				}
704				if tip != landSHA {
705					return fmt.Errorf("expected main at %s, got %s", landSHA, tip)
706				}
707				return nil
708			})
709		}()
710	
711		// Wait for the second worker to complete.
712		select {
713		case err := <-secondWorkerErr:
714			require.NoError(t, err, "second worker should proceed after stale lock recovery")
715		case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
716			t.Fatal("second worker did not complete in time")
717		}
718	
719		// Verify the lock was cleaned up after the second worker.
720		if _, err := os.Stat(lockDir); !os.IsNotExist(err) {
721			t.Fatalf("lock dir should be cleaned up after second worker: stat err = %v", err)
722		}
723	}
